subject: grammar
title: Modals: Travel Advice Dialogue
instruction: Fill in the gaps with the correct form of (not) have to, ought to or must(n't) and the verbs in brackets.
passage:
Teacher: Do you think it's a good thing for young people to travel to different countries before settling down to a job?
Student: Yes, I think it's a really exciting and interesting thing to do.
Teacher: What 1 [1] (you/think) about if you're going to go travelling?
Student: Well, you 2 [2] (consider) lots of things first. For example, you
3 [3] (have) enough money in the first place, so you
4 [4] (work) a bit first to save some money. Then another
important thing to consider is who to go with. You 5 [5] (travel)
with a friend, but it is probably safer and less lonely if you do. Also, you
6 [6] (learn) a bit about the countries before you go. It's a good
idea to research cultural issues, so that you don't offend people by your
behaviour. If you go to Nepal, for example, you 7 [7] (shout) or
raise your voice in public, and you 8 [8] (always/walk) around a
Buddhist temple in a clockwise direction. You 9 [9] (find out)
these things before you go.
Teacher: What things 10 [10] (you/arrange) before you travel?
Student: Well, to visit some countries you 11 [11] (have) a visa, so you
12 [12] (organise) that before you go.
